title: Compute the Ti/Tv ratio of a VCF
description: Compute the transition-to-transversion ratio for a 20-variant VCF. Transitions are purine<->purine (A<->G) and pyrimidine<->pyrimidine (C<->T) substitutions; transversions are everything else. Tests numeric output with tolerance.
task_prompt: You are given a VCF file at sample_titv.vcf in your working directory. Compute the Ti/Tv ratio: the count of transition substitutions divided by the count of transversion substitutions across all variant records. Transitions are A<->G and C<->T. Transversions are A<->C, A<->T, G<->C, G<->T. Consider only single-nucleotide substitutions (REF and ALT both one base). Write your answer as JSON to eval_answer.json with this exact schema: {"titv_ratio": <number>}. Report at least 3 decimal places.
notes: 12 transitions (A<->G x6, C<->T x6), 8 transversions (A<->T, A<->C, G<->C, G<->T pairs). True ratio 12/8 = 1.5 exactly. Absolute tolerance 0.01 allows for off-by-one rounding.
